sessions.details: recorded 1980-10-29 for Friday Rock Show. Debut TX. 'Sweet And Innocent' and 'Lightning To The Nations' available on The Friday Rock Show Sessions (Raw Fruit).

recorded 1980-10-06 for John Peel (Session #5). First TX 1980-10-20. All except 'Hit Or Miss' available on Sessions Of The Damned (Strange Fruit). 'Hit Or Miss' available on The Radio One Sessions (Nighttracks).

track_listing.details: LP-The Best Of Emerson, Lake & Palmer; Ariola

track_listing.track: 'Can't Stop It But I'm Gonna Try (LP-All Shook Up)' (Epic)
'Rock And Roll Ain't Noise Pollution (LP-Back In Black)' (Atlantic)
'Can't Say No To You (LP-Stand Up And Fight)' (MCA)

transcript.text: On 275 and 285. Also in stereo on VHF. This is National Radio 1.
Oh, hello there. This is TV on the Radio here, Thomas the Vance, and welcome to a very warming Friday Rock Show.
And tonight on our program we have the first ever session by a young rock band from Worcestershire.
They're all age 20 and they have the name Diamondhead.
Also, we have a recent set by The Damned that might surprise you.
And we've got some classic cuts by Iron Maiden, Alex Harvey, Roger Daughtry, Deep Purple, Black Sabbath, and others.
Some new stuff by Cheap Trade, Talking Heads, Weapon, ACDC, Witchfind, Quartz, and the Eagles.
Not forgetting our Friday Night Connection, the answer to last week's and a new one.
But this evening, we start with this.
Welcome back, my friends, to the show that never ends.
We're so glad you could attend.
Come inside, come inside.
There behind the glass, there's a real blade of grass.
Be careful as you pass
Move along, move along
Come inside, the show's about to start
Guaranteed to blow your head off
That's a track by Cheap Trick.
It's from their new album, which is called All Shook Up.
And the track I played you was Can't Stop It But I'm Gonna Try.
And before that, something from a new compilation
that's just come out on Atlantic Records.
It's a compilation featuring the best of Emerson, Lake and Palmer,
as the compiler sees it.
The track you heard was Carn Evil First Impression Part 2.
It's a good compilation, it's also got a hoedown on it
Lucky Man, Still You Turn Me On
Peter Gunn, Jerusalem, Trilogy
Fanfare for the Common Man, Tiger in a Spotlight
So if you like all those tracks
You can get them on that compilation
And I played that track, Carnival First Impression
Especially for Muff and Frank and Perky
And John, I think it is, J-U-N
John, maybe, John, definitely
Dab, Mick, Helen, Peace and Love to Everybody
Including T.S. McPhee, The LCC
CND, Greenpeace
And Somebody Called Caroline
And that card came from Dumbarton
Also, we played ELP for S. Atkinson, who lives in Sheffield.
Thirteen minutes after ten, here's the first of four by Diamondhead.
And that was Iron Maiden.
And that's a track from their debut album called Iron Maiden.
It's been out for quite a while.
The track was Prowler.
Played that for loads of people.
I'll just mention the people who signed on the bottom of the cards and letters.
David and Patrick Trigwell, who live in Carnforth in Lancashire.
David Leitch, he's in Leamington Spa.
Diane Davies, who's in Southampton in Hampshire.
Dave and Mark, Chadwell Heath in Essex
Ian Nuttall, who lives in Collingdale
just up the road in London, NWI
Robert Anderson of Bernard Castle School
Bernard Castle in County Durham
Dee, who's a lady who lives in Derby
and also Joy, who's another lady
who lives, would you believe, in Derby
All those people wanted to hear Iron Maiden
and I hope they enjoyed Prowler
Performed at the first of four from our session tonight
the first session they've ever done for the BBC
Diamondhead are the name of the band
The track was Sweet and Innocent
You'll also find that out as their current single
It's 10.21 on the Friday Rock Show
And now I'm going to play you three records in a row
Talking Heads, The Damned, and I'm sure you'll recognize the third
You may find yourself in a beautiful house
With a beautiful wife
Ask yourself
A sensational Alex Harvey band
And a track called Faith Healer
That was for a gentleman by the name of Mike
who lives in Menzies Hill in Dundee in Scotland.
I want to mention Thog and Jake and Phil
and all the other bozos from Dundee.
That's his word, not mine.
Also played there for a gentleman by the name of Peter.
Peter Everett, that is.
Best wishes, Peter, for this coming Monday,
the 1st of December.
You're getting engaged, I'm told,
to your childhood sweetheart, Chrissie Metcalf.
Your dad, Mr. NF Everett,
sends his love to you.
And very best wishes.
Also, I've got to give a mention
to all the friends of Peter
who attended the De La Salle High School.
Everett.
Now, that's a good Liverpool name.
I tell you what, Peter, if you and Chrissy have a boy, you should call him Kenny.
Before that, you heard something by our session from the dam,
whom I think are sort of incredible in as much as they work in heavy rock and punk and everything together.
The track we played was Therapy, and that was for Filthy Animal Smythe and Fast Paddy and Dirty Darren
and all the other headbangers, the skull-busting freaks of Wallace High School,
Liz Vernon, can't he, over there in Northern Ireland?
I can't remember what county it's in, but I know it well.
Prior to that, something from the album by Talking Heads.
A good album. It's called Remain in Light.
The track we play was called Once in a Lifetime.
The time now is 21 and a half minutes to 11.
And Diamond Head.
That's what I call a tight little record.
That is actually a B-side.
The B-side of a single by a band called Weapon.
On the A-side, it's a mad, mad world.
The B-side you just heard was Set the Stage Alight.
I was actually given that by a gentleman who purchased it from a record store in Birmingham
when I was up there at Bogart's a few weeks ago.
They sound like a really good band.
They're on the WEP label, whatever that is,
or where indeed you can find it, I don't know,
but I think it is in the shops.
Before that, you heard something by Diamondhead,
our session band tonight.
It was Lightning to the Nations,
and they're a darn good band from Worcestershire.
Play that little selection for...
I'll read this letter, actually.
We of the Dillam Hall Headbanging Society
would like to dedicate a rock record to our housemaster,
whose musical tastes E.G. Beethoven and Mozart
out are in desperate need of reform. As we quiver in ourselves with our radios as our
only contact with the outside world, the playing of a good track would keep us going until
our parole at Christmas. We put our wretched six-form souls in your hands, yours pathetically.
Crapper Pope, pardon, Malfunction Millington, Bubonic Bert, Jack Rigamortis Ward, and O
de Tarros, whatever that means. They're all at Love Low in Shropshire. The Diamondhead
track was specially for Jeremy Ashby, who is a very dedicated Diamondhead fan.
13 minutes to 11, and I do hope you're wrapped up nice and warm.
You can spend the next 10 minutes and 20 seconds
taking Curtain Call Part 1 with The Damned.
...being one of the greatest works ever.
Amazing piece of music.
It's called Curtain Call Part One.
It's from a session that was recorded on Monday the 6th of October
in the BBC Studios by The Damned.
Dave Vanian, or sometimes, if you like, Vanian on vocals,
Raskavy's on the drums,
Catherine Sensible on the guitar and keyboards,
and Paul Grey on the bass.
Produced by Tony Wilson.
A magic moment.
Two minutes to eleven.
This is the Friday Rock Show.
It's time for the Friday Night Connection.
Who's won it this week?
Well, I'll tell you in just a second,
but first let me remind you of the tracks that I played you last week.
we played the red telephone by love from the album forever changes we played young lust by
the pink floyds from the album called the wall then we played filing dale flyer by jethro tarr
the red telephone the telephone call at the end of young lust and filing dale flyer is all about
that place where they watch out for invaders with nuclear bombs within which is of course
a telephone. Consequently, the answer was telephone. The winner is Dave Miller of number
10, Creswell Street, Kings Lynn in Norfolk. Now, older viewers to this program might say,
but he's won before. Yes, he has won before, but he writes in every single week. And I
think his connection and answer are worthy of another ten-pound record token. And I
think you will when you hear the music that he's put together. Dave, congratulations. And
here is Mr. Miller's Friday Night Connection. Three bits of music with a connection or
theme contained therein listen can't you hear me say can't you hear me say 10 past 11 and that was
it the friday night connection put together by dave miller kingsland norfolk three bits of music
somewhere hidden therein there is a theme mr dave miller suggests though i think some of you might
be a little bit foxed then it makes it all the more interesting doesn't it if you think you know
the answer jot it down on a postcard letter anything you like send it to tommy vance the
friday night connection the friday rock show bbc radio one london w1a4ww okay the winner gets a 10
pound record token but don't forget put together your own friday night connection as well diamond
head are living on borrowed time rock and roll and noise pollution it's not often that we play
chart records but that is in the charts it's gone straight in this week at number 23 it's the latest
a single by ACDC, and it features, of course, the amazing voice of Brian Johnson, who was
a guest last week on Rock on Saturday, and what a great Geordie lad he is. Prior to that,
you heard something from our session by Diamondhead. The track was Borrowed Time. Now, Diamondhead
are from Stourbridge in Worcestershire, and they're made up of Brian Tattler on the guitar,
Colin Kimberley on the bass, Duncan Scott on the drums, and Sean Harris on the vocals.
And you can find an album of theirs on Media Records, which is a company that's based
in Wolverhampton and for my money they're going to be very big in 1981. Here's something you'll
recognize but you might not know this version. The album's called Stage Fright, the band are
called Witchfind and the track you just heard was Trick. I haven't really gotten into that album yet
but I've just heard it sort of playing in the background a couple of three times. I think it
sounds like a tasty one. I'm going to take it home, play it and listen to it in the cans, stereo
And I think I'll be a satisfied man after I've heard it.
Ah, before that, you heard Deep Purple from a new double album,
which has the title Deep Purple in Concert.
The track, of course, was Highway Star.
Let me tell you something about this album.
It's just come out on Harvest Records, right?
And it features two BBC concerts.
One recorded in 1970, the other one in 1972.
Highway Star was actually recorded in 72.
A lot of people have been after this material for a long, long time.
It's not been available in this form,
although I think a few people have had it on tape for some time, haven't they?
Nod, nod, wink, wink.
And now you can get it on record.
It's a double album, and it's out on Harvest,
and it is called Deep Purple in Concert.
And looking at the sleeve notes,
I noticed that Tony Wilson, for the 1970 concert,
was actually the engineer.
Oh, it's not just a pretty face, you know.
A little bit of a beef about the album.
It states on the album,
it's a Heck Enterprises original sound recording.
That's right, it was recorded at the BBC
I mean, let's face it, credit where credit's due
And a lot of credit was due to a lot of people in the BBC
For putting that bad on the air in that period
It's 25 minutes to midnight, well, I'll be damned
Bit of a classic, Black Sabbath, Paranoid
For loads of people, as always, because we get so many requests for Black Sabbath
I'll just read a few out
Stuart Grantham's got a 19th birthday on the 15th of November
Happy birthday from Mum, Dad and Maureen
All living in Middle Barton in Oxfordshire
Know it well, lovely part of the world
Licky Knox wanted me to play that for everybody in the fifth year at Smith Hills Moore Grammar School.
That's in Bolton in Lancashire.
I also had to play it for Sandra, from a gentleman who's having a bit of a holiday on the Isle of Wight.
And for all other Sabbath fans as well.
Before that you heard Hit or Miss from our session by the band, which I hope you enjoy.
Nineteen to Midnight, Diamond Head, Don't You Ever Leave Me.
What a rockin' band they are, that's Quartz from their album Stand Up and Fight.
The track was Can't Say No to You.
A good man from Birmingham.
Moseley School is in Birmingham, as if you didn't know.
And I've been asked to say hello to Andy and the two Daves and Paddy and Chids and Colin and our only girl Jackie, it says here.
All the Moseley sixth form rockers.
Right, and also it was for a lady by the name of Alison, about whom I know nothing, but I guess somebody out there does.
The track you heard before that was Diamond Head.
And they did a session for us tonight which I thought was absolutely spectacular.
well produced by tony wilson and extremely well played by that young band from worcestershire
that you heard was don't you ever leave me we're gonna leave you with one track that has the title
life in the fast lane but just before i play it i'd like to remind you that tonight there's a lot
of black ice about on the roads if you're in the fast lane slow down and take it easy yours from
the album eagles live a life in the fast lane and that's it for this edition of the friday rock show
Hope you enjoyed Diamond Head and also The Damned
On Rock on Saturday tomorrow between 4 and 7.30
You'll hear John Anderson, P.I.L. and the Climax Blues Band in concert
Which is mighty tasty
My engineer tonight was Keith
Following the time term for midnight
The 275-285 transmitters will be closing down
And now with more airtime
Here's Tony Wilson, the producer of this program
Tony
The Friday Rock Show is produced by us just for you
Oh, this is like an advert
Yeah
The Friday Rock Show is produced by us just for you
And it is.
Wherever you are in our rather cold country, take care of yourselves.
If you're driving tonight, take extra care.
We'd hate to lose you.
And I mean that.
I'll see you tomorrow at four o'clock.
From me, Tommy Vance, good night and God bless.
